comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented (commit d8edc01df). build_payload carries session_id+deps off the verdict; reviewed_file_hashes reuses attest._hash_file (absent sentinel); _cited_paths_code_review parses location; deps computed unconditionally in _run_code_review_gate before emit; latest_code_review_result reader (typed session/change keyspaces, surfaced=blocking+advisory, best-effort/schema-guarded). Local: ruff+mypy green, 47 code-review tests pass. Awaiting Gerrit Verified before close.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Code review reviews a DIFF fresh with NO memory across runs. The durable `code_review`
artifact (per Gerrit change/revision) today persists SURFACED findings write-only (a
calibration corpus). This story adds the READER + the content-addressed STATE the region-gated
novelty floor (blameless-grindable-noctule) needs, and extends the `code_review_result_v1`
payload with the two new fields that state.

## What
- **Payload extension (`code_review_result_v1`, `build_payload`).** Add two fields, both read
  off the verdict dict so they are populated once and flow to EVERY emit path unchanged:
  - `session_id`: TOP-LEVEL, nullable. `build_payload` reads `verdict.get("session_id")`
    (None for Gerrit reviews; set for local session-keyed reviews by the persistence story
    paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish). Makes local memory queryable by session.
  - `deps`: a `{reviewed-file path: sha256}` content-hash map. `build_payload` reads
    `verdict.get("deps")` (defaulting to `{}`).
- **deps collector + computation seam (`produce_code_review_verdict`).** A thin collector hashes
  each reviewed-file path once and stashes the map on the verdict (`verdict["deps"]`) in
  `_run_code_review_gate`, computed UNCONDITIONALLY after the verdict is produced and BEFORE the
  sidecar emit. This requires importing `code_review.sidecar` (for the collector +
  `_cited_paths_code_review`) EARLIER in `_run_code_review_gate` — at the top of the finalize block
  rather than only inside the existing `if request.target_ticket:` block (an explicit new import in
  `gate_dispatch.py`, enumerated here). Because it lives on the verdict dict, BOTH
  emit callers pick it up via `build_payload` with NO change to either call site:
  - the produce-path emit (`_run_code_review_gate` → `sidecar.emit`), and
  - the Gerrit voter emit (`voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` → `sidecar.emit`), which passes
    the SAME verdict object it received — so it inherits `deps`/`session_id` for free (voter.py
    stays OUT of scope; it needs no edit).
  - **Reuse boundary:** the collector REUSES the private `attest._hash_file(path, base=…)`
    primitive (raw file-bytes sha256; a missing/unreadable path → the `absent` sentinel, so a
    create/delete is itself detectable) and `attest._hash_basis(repo_root)` for the base. This
    reuse of a private symbol is EXPLICITLY sanctioned by the epic design ("private `_hash_file`
    primitive reused; NOT `dependency_hashes`, which is plan/ticket-coupled"). It does NOT reuse
    `dependency_hashes()` (that pulls ticket file_impact + plan-verdict citation buckets).
  - **Scope of `deps`** = the review's `changed_files` UNION its cited-file paths. NOTE:
    `attest._cited_paths(verdict)` is NOT reusable here — it reads a `citations` list with
    `kind == "file"`, but code-review findings carry no such list (they only have a `location`
    string, e.g. `path` or `path:line`). So define a small `_cited_paths_code_review(verdict)`
    in `code_review/sidecar.py` that extracts the FILE path (strip any `:line` suffix) from each
    finding's `location` across the `blocking` + `advisory` buckets, ignoring empty/non-file
    locations. `deps` = `sorted(set(changed_files) | _cited_paths_code_review(verdict))`.
  - **Basis:** hashed via `attest._hash_basis(repo_root)` at the emit point. Because deps are
    computed AFTER the workflow's `gate_read_root` context has exited, `current_code_root()` is
    already `None`, so `_hash_basis` resolves to the in-place WORKING TREE (`_config.repo_root`) —
    NOT the attested snapshot. This is correct for both keyspaces: a LOCAL `review-code` reviews the
    working tree (so the working tree IS the reviewed basis), and the Gerrit voter bot checks out
    the patchset revision (so its working tree IS the reviewed revision). The content-addressed
    region gate only ever compares hashes WITHIN one keyspace, and each keyspace hashes its own
    working-tree basis consistently across runs, which is all it needs.
  - **Failure posture:** the collector is best-effort — a per-file hash failure yields the
    `absent` sentinel (never raises); a total failure leaves `deps` empty and is logged. It must
    NEVER fail the gate (mirrors the sidecar's best-effort posture).
- **Artifact addressing (title schemes, TYPED keyspaces).** Local artifacts use title
  `code-review: session:{session_id}`; Gerrit artifacts keep `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`
  (unchanged). These are two disjoint keyspaces so a prior LOCAL review can never seed a change's
  FIRST Gerrit review.
- **Reader `latest_code_review_result(key, *, repo_root=None)`.** `key` is a TYPED string:
  `session:<id>` or `change:<id>`. Resolution by artifact title:
  - `session:<id>` → the artifact whose title == `code-review: session:{id}` (exact; one artifact).
  - `change:<id>` → STRIP the `change:` tag to get the bare `change_id`, then match artifacts whose
    title starts with `code-review: {change_id} @ ` (spans revisions of the same change; the change
    is the memory key, the revision only makes each artifact idempotent). An unknown key kind → None.
  Artifacts are enumerated via `rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")` and matched by title
  in-process (no new list-filter API). Across the matched artifact(s), the reader returns the
  most-recent usable `code_review_result_v1` sidecar payload's SURFACED findings + its `deps` map.
  - **SURFACED-only (mandatory; see old-frilly-plankton).** `code_review_result_v1` stores findings
    in SEPARATE top-level buckets (`blocking`, `advisory`, `coaching`) with NO per-finding
    `decision` field (unlike `plan_review_result_v1`). Therefore "surfaced" is DEFINED as the union
    of the `blocking` and `advisory` buckets; the reader returns ONLY those two buckets (never
    `coaching`, never any future dropped bucket). Dropped findings are never persisted into
    blocking/advisory, so they can never re-enter the novelty prior set.
  - **Posture:** mirrors `plan_review.sidecar.latest_review_result` — best-effort, NEVER raises,
    schema-guarded (only `code_review_result_v1` payloads), walks newest→oldest over each artifact's
    sidecar events tolerating a malformed/foreign newest file, returns None when nothing usable.
    A reader/hash/compare error degrades to None (⇒ the floor runs un-narrowed = no drops).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — payload extension (`build_payload`), the thin deps
  collector, and the `latest_code_review_result` reader.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` — the `deps` computation seam in
  `produce_code_review_verdict` / `_run_code_review_gate` (stash on the verdict before emit).
- OUT of scope: local persistence wiring / setting `session_id` (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish);
  the region_gated_floor consumer (blameless-grindable-noctule); `voter.py` (inherits via the
  verdict — no edit).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `build_payload` adds a nullable top-level `session_id` (read from `verdict.get("session_id")`)
      and a `deps` `{path: sha256}` map (read from `verdict.get("deps")`, default `{}`).
- [ ] A thin deps collector reuses `attest._hash_file` (NOT `dependency_hashes`) and the `absent`
      sentinel; scope = `changed_files` ∪ `_cited_paths_code_review(verdict)` (paths parsed from
      each finding's `location`, since code-review findings have no `citations[kind==file]` list).
- [ ] The deps map is computed ONCE in `produce_code_review_verdict`, UNCONDITIONALLY (regardless of
      `target_ticket`), and stashed on the verdict — importing `code_review.sidecar` earlier in
      `_run_code_review_gate` — so both the produce emit and the Gerrit voter emit carry it via
      `build_payload` (voter unchanged).
- [ ] `latest_code_review_result(key)` resolves `session:<id>` (exact title `code-review: session:{id}`)
      and `change:<id>` (strip `change:`, title prefix `code-review: {id} @`); unknown kind → None.
- [ ] The reader returns SURFACED findings = union of the payload's `blocking` + `advisory` buckets
      (never `coaching`/dropped) plus the `deps` map; schema-guarded to `code_review_result_v1`.
- [ ] The reader is best-effort and NEVER raises; malformed/absent/foreign-schema → None (⇒ no drops).
- [ ] Tests: payload carries `session_id` + `deps`; deps collector hashes changed+cited paths with
      the `absent` sentinel for a missing file; reader resolves both typed keyspaces, returns
      surfaced-only, and degrades to None on a malformed/absent artifact.

file_impact.reason: payload session_id+deps, deps collector, latest_code_review_result reader
deps computation seam stashes deps on verdict before emit

compiled_state.title: Code-review finding-memory reader + artifact payload (session_id + reviewed-file content-hash map)
